The largest upward move occurred on 2026-01-26, with a 28.91% increase closing at 6.8, coinciding with several news items including a spike in natural gas prices to a 3.25-year high and first quarter fiscal 2026 earnings reports from National Fuel, which may have contributed to this surge [1][2]. Another notable large upward day was on 2026-01-20, with a 25.9104% gain closing at 3.907, accompanied by high volume of 410,520 shares, during a period marked by a historic 25% daily surge in natural gas markets driven by an Arctic blast [3]. The largest downward move was on 2026-01-29, with a -47.4799% drop closing at 3.918, followed by another significant decline on 2026-02-02 of -25.6546% closing at 3.237, which was also a high volume day with 554,857 shares traded [4]. The average daily volume was 188,588 shares, with a volume decrease of -10.5154% over the period, suggesting some reduction in trading activity. The price movements and volume spikes appear to be closely linked to natural gas market dynamics and weather-related demand changes, as reflected in multiple news releases and market updates during late January and early February 2026 [1][3][4].
tldr: Highest volume spike was on 2026-02-02 with volume 554,857.
